analysis.summary: Low-risk extraction refactor. The plan moves inline point generation logic (lines 47-130 of pointCloud.ts) into a dedicated module with five shape variants. The GeometrySystem interface is unchanged, ModeManager integration is unaffected, and the existing shader pipeline remains intact. The new generators/ directory establishes a clean separation between geometry generation and system orchestration.
positives: Clean extraction: pointCloud.ts init() currently hard-codes a 60/40 shell/interior distribution in ~80 lines; extracting to a dedicated module improves testability and opens future reuse by ribbonField or new systems
No interface changes: GeometrySystem interface (init/draw/cleanup/setOpacity) is untouched; ModeManager, renderLoop, and main.ts require zero modifications
PRNG compatibility: createPRNG from src/visual/prng.ts already supports seed suffixes and is used identically across all three systems; new generators can use it directly
Existing test assertions (Z-depth > 1.0, non-coplanar, determinism) directly validate the acceptance criteria without needing new integration test infrastructure
Post-processing preserved: lattice-snapping and softness-modulation remain as post-processing on generated positions, maintaining visual continuity

concerns.issue: Shape selection determinism must use the same PRNG stream as existing generation to avoid changing visual output for existing seeds
concerns.mitigation: Plan step 3 specifies seeded choice among five types; the existing shell/sphere distribution should map to one of the five generators (sphereVolume or shell) to preserve backward compatibility for current seeds

concerns.issue: The noiseLattice generator uses 'sine-based displacement' on a 3D grid which may produce visually distinct density patterns compared to the current spherical distribution
concerns.mitigation: All generators are tested for volumetric spread; visual variety is the artistic intent

missingConsiderations: Plan does not mention whether existing seeds should produce identical output post-refactor (backward compatibility) or if visual change is acceptable since the experience is ephemeral
No mention of re-exporting the generator module or updating barrel exports if the project uses them

patternsFollowed: Factory function pattern consistent with existing systems (createPointCloud, createRibbonField, createParticleField)
Seeded determinism via createPRNG with unique suffixes
GPU-driven rendering with static base positions and shader-based deformation
Pure function generators returning Float32Array match the buffer-update-over-object-churn guidance

coverageGaps: No performance/benchmark tests for generators with high point counts (maxPoints=1200)
No visual regression tests, but this is consistent with the existing test strategy

suggestions: Consider making the current 60/40 shell/interior distribution one of the named generators (e.g., 'sphereVolume') so that seed-backward-compatibility is achievable if desired

The generator function signature should accept the PRNG function rather than a seed string, keeping PRNG creation in the caller for consistency with how pointCloud.ts already manages its PRNG instance
